titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 556

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=19 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:19:35.822744+00 | translation moa=20 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:19:35.841127+00

p.cl: Eadger . rex scottorum  Omnibus per regnum suum scottis & anglis  salutem .

Sciatis me ad dedicationem uenisse ęcclesię Sanctę Marię apud
Coldingaham . quę quidem dedicatio ad dei laudem & ad meum placitum grata omnibus & accepta honorabiliter est adimpleta . & ego
eidem ęcclesię super altare obtuli in dotem . & donaui uillam totam Swintun cum diuisis sicut liulf habuit  liberam & quietam inperpetuum
habendam ab omni calumpnia . & ad uoluntatem Monachorum Sancti CVThBERTI dispondendam . pro animabus patris & matris meę
& pro salute animę meę & fratrum & sororum mearum . Donaui etiam monachis . xxiiii . animalia ad restaurandam illam eandem
terram . & constitui eandem pacem in Coldingaham eundo & redeundo & ibidem manendo . quę seruatur in Eilande . ⁊ in Norcham . Insuper
etiam statui hominibus in Coldingamscire sicut ipsi elegerunt & in manu mea firmauerunt . ut unoquoque anno de unaquaque car
ruca . dimidaim marcam argenti monachis persoluant .

T. Ælfw’. oter . ⁊ Thor long’ . ⁊ Ælfric’ pincerna . ⁊ Algaro presbitero
⁊ Osberno presbitero . ⁊ Cnut carles sune . ⁊ Ogga ⁊ Lesing . ⁊ Swein ulfkilles sune . ⁊ Ligulf de bebbanburch . ⁊ Vhtred Eilaues sune
Uuiæt hwite . ⁊ Tigerne .
Edgar king of the Scots to all Scots and English throughout his kingdom greeting.

Know me to have come to the consecration of the church of St Mary at Coldingham. This dedication was fulfilled to the glory of God and to my pleasing, was acceptable to all and honourably received. And I have offered to the same church upon the altar as an endowment and have donated the whole toun of Swinton with the boundaries just as Liulf had, to be had in perpetuity free and quit from every claim, and to be disposed at the will of the monks of Saint Cuthbert; for the souls of my father and mother, and for the salvation of my own soul and my brothers’ and sisters’. I have also donated to the monks 24 beasts to re-stock that same land. And I have established the same peace in Coldingham, to those going and returning and abiding there, which is preserved in Island and in Norham. I have moreover also established with the men in Coldinghamshire, just as they themselves have chosen and have confirmed in my hand, that they they should pay every year for each plough half a merk of silver to the monks.

As witnesses: Ælfwine, Óttar, and Thor Long, and Ælfric the butler, and Algar the priest, and Osbern the priest, and Cnut Carl’s son, and Oggu and Leising, and Swain Ulfkill’s son, and Liulf of Bamburgh, and Uhtred Eilaf’s son, Uviet White, and Tigerna.
